status_reason: Both API models ran (second full-set run). GPT-5.4 Pro (reasoning_effort: high, 1497s, all 5 passing hypotheses). Gemini 3.1 Pro (thinking: HIGH, 39s, all 5 passing hypotheses).

H1.4.gemini_depth: Formal isomorphism (Nernst/mass-action equations identical across mineral and protein-phase Cu-Fe-S chemistry; Irving-Williams as conserved topological invariant)
H1.4.combined_recommendation: HIGH PRIORITY — reframe from Ksp proof to protein-context Cu(I)-Fe-S injury; run purified protein anaerobic Cu(I) challenge panel (LIAS/ACO2 vs IRP1/ABCE1)

H1.2.gemini_depth: Structural correspondence (Marcus-Hush theory — FDX1 homomorphically maps to mineral surface catalysis via reorganization energy λ reduction; near-isoenergetic transfer prediction)
H1.2.combined_recommendation: PROMISING — run FDX1-KO + mitochondrial LplA lipoylation bypass + elesclomol to discriminate copper-gate vs lipoylation-enabler role; decisive single experiment in session

H1.3.gemini_depth: Metaphorical similarity — Bendixson criterion proves no limit cycle exists (oscillator rejected by mathematics); CuS precipitation is a monostable dampening node
H1.3.combined_recommendation: NEEDS REFRAMING — drop oscillator framing; reframe as irreversible CuS sink with biphasic viability test as distinguishing prediction

H1.1.gemini_depth: Structural correspondence — ring-strain thermodynamics (ΔG_bind = ΔG_electronic + ΔG_strain) formally accounts for 2-order binding gap; DFT + ITC verification approach specified
H1.1.combined_recommendation: PROMISING for targeted ITC panel — DFT ring-strain + 1,2-dithiolane vs 1,3-dithiol vs monothiol Cu+ binding; drop molecular fossil narrative

H1.7.gemini_depth: Formal homomorphism — Pourbaix state vector maps to evolutionary fitness function; I(E; S) mutual information between GTDB environment metadata and FDX1 D136/D139 sequence variants is quantitative test
H1.7.combined_recommendation: NEEDS WORK — phylogenetically corrected comparative genomics first; delay ASR; GPT raises specific geochemical objections: vent sulfide precipitates Cu, Cu2+ scarce in reducing environments, LIAS-ferredoxin coupling explained by radical-SAM biochemistry

key_divergences: H1.2: widest gap in session — Gemini 9/10 Marcus-theory structural correspondence vs GPT 4/10 empirical (FDX1 lipoylation enabler is stronger published explanation; free lipoic acid is wrong ligand proxy; Marcus theory misapplied to ligand exchange not outer-sphere ET; ΔΨm threshold not derivable from Pourbaix alone)

H1.7: Gemini 8/10 formal homomorphism vs GPT 2/10 empirical (vent sulfide precipitates Cu reducing bioavailability; Cu2+ scarce in reducing vents making Cu2+ reductase selection pressure poorly motivated; LIAS-ferredoxin coupling has simpler radical-SAM explanation)
key_agreements: H1.4: both models affirm Nernst/mass-action thermodynamic bridge is formally real; Cu(I) attack on exposed Fe-S clusters precedented; agreed first experiment is purified protein anaerobic challenge panel

H1.2: both confirm absolute novelty (no Pourbaix analysis of intracellular Cu published); both confirm thermodynamic redundancy paradox means model must shift to purely kinetic and requires direct rate measurements
H1.7: both agree comparative genomics (GTDB + phylogenetic correction) is correct first step before any wet-lab investment
H1.3: Bendixson criterion formally rejects oscillator model — self-terminating by mathematics given ETC disruption reduces H2O2
No fabricated citations detected: GPT confirmed PMID 37225108 (ISCA1/2 2023) correctly cited; 2025 citations treated as context
cross_cutting_issue: Equilibrium Pourbaix logic applied to protein-dominated non-equilibrium mitochondrial matrix. Free Cu(I) is sub-attomolar (fully protein/thiol-buffered per Rae et al. 1999). Conditional potentials derived from biological ligand mixture (GSH, protein thiolates, protein-bound lipoyl-lysine — not free lipoic acid) are required. This is the single most important technical correction for both H1.4 and H1.2 before any PHREEQC computation.
priority_ranking: H1.4 — HIGH PRIORITY (GPT:6, Gemini:9; immediate bench work; reframe Ksp argument; purified protein panel)
H1.2 — PROMISING (GPT:4, Gemini:9; most decisive single experiment: FDX1-KO + LplA bypass)
H1.1 — PROMISING for targeted chemistry (Gemini:7; DFT + ITC panel; self-contained)
H1.7 — NEEDS WORK (GPT:2, Gemini:8; computational first; GPT geochemical objections require addressing)
H1.3 — REFRAME (oscillator rejected by Bendixson; reframe as irreversible sink)
